seoTitle: How to Get a 30-Day Free Trial on TradingView Premium
metaDescription: Find out how to activate a 30-day TradingView free trial for Pro, Pro+, or Premium, plus eligibility rules and how to cancel before billing starts.
intro: TradingView's premium plans unlock extra indicators, chart layouts, and faster data, and a free trial is the easiest way to test them before paying. This guide explains how to activate a 30-day trial for Pro, Pro+, or Premium, along with the eligibility rules that can block it. It also covers how to cancel before billing kicks in so you're not charged unexpectedly.

steps.heading: Check trial eligibility
steps.body: Confirm you meet TradingView's eligibility rules for the 30-day trial before starting the activation process, since some accounts don't qualify.

steps.heading: Activate the free trial
steps.body: Choose the Pro, Pro+, or Premium plan you want to try and activate the 30-day trial through your TradingView account settings.

steps.heading: Explore premium features during the trial
steps.body: Use the trial period to test premium tools like extra indicators, advanced layouts, and alerts to see which plan fits your needs.

steps.heading: Cancel before billing to avoid charges
steps.body: If you don't want to continue after the trial, cancel before the 30 days end so you aren't automatically billed.

faq.question: Does TradingView offer a 30-day free trial in 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, TradingView offers a 30-day trial for its Pro, Pro+, and Premium plans, though eligibility rules apply.

faq.question: Will I be charged automatically after the trial ends?
faq.answer: Yes, unless you cancel before the trial period ends, billing typically starts automatically.

faq.question: What features are unlocked during the trial?
faq.answer: The trial gives access to premium features like additional indicators, advanced chart layouts, and alerts.

faq.question: What might block me from getting the free trial?
faq.answer: Certain eligibility rules and account history can prevent an account from qualifying for the trial, so it's worth checking before assuming you're covered.
